free_text: Study of activation cross-sections of deuteron induced
reactions on erbium: Production of radioisotopes for
practical applications
Preliminary data. Proceedings of
9th International Symposium on the Synthesis and
Applications of Isotopes and Isotopically Labelled
Compounds, 16-20 July 2006, Edinburgh, UK.
Commercial (Goodfellow) high purity Er foils,
thicknesses: 25 and 32 micron.
Primary deuteron energies: 40 MeV and 20 MeV,
beam intensities: 88.6 and 110 nA,
irradiation time: 30 and 80 min,
counting started: about 7-8 h and 2-3 h after the end
of bombardment at the Tohoku University and at the VUB,
respectively.
Nondestructive measurement without chemical separation.
Ti: 12 and 31 micron, Al: 100 micron.
Chapter 4
The energy degradation along the stack was determined
by calculation on the basis of Andersen and Ziegler.
Decay data were taken from NuDat2 database.
Estimated combining the uncertainties of the
energy degradation depending on the thickness of the
target foils in the stack and energy straggling.
Error sources:
* number of the bombarding particles (7%),
* number of the target nuclei (3%),
* decay data (3%),
* detector efficiency (7%),
* peak area (1-10%).
The linearly contributing independent processes were
taken into account extracting square root of
quadratically summed relative uncertainties.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the code ALICE-IPPE.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections integral yields were calculated.

To avoid overlapping and contamination from the
84.94 keV Kb1 X-ray line from gamma-ray excitation of
Pb, present in the normal shielding of the
spectrometric set-up, separate measurements were
carried out with an X-ray detector in air, far from any
lead material. In spite of these precautions the
different X-rays of lead still appear in the spectra
but with very low intensity.
A correction for the contamination at the 84.3 keV
energy of interest was performed on the basis of the
well separated 74.97 keV Ka1 X-ray line of Pb.
Data were taken from Table 2 of the ref.
